_meta.description: Validation policies for accounting domain — correctness checks, consistency guards, and quality gates.

policies.description: Total debits must equal total credits in any journal entry or trial balance.
Flag accounts with balances opposite to their normal balance (e.g., credit balance in an asset account).
Adjusted book balance must equal adjusted bank balance after all reconciling items.
Transactions must be posted within the correct accounting period. Flag out-of-period postings.
Referenced account codes should exist in the chart of accounts.
Subledger totals (AR, AP) must agree with the corresponding GL control account.
Sum of aging buckets must equal the total balance reported.
Manual or adjusting journal entries above materiality threshold should show authorization evidence.

Debit and credit sign convention must be explicit and consistent: either (a) debit and credit columns are unsigned positive values, or (b) a single signed amount column uses debit as positive and credit as negative with indicator support.
Rollforward schedules must apply sign direction consistently (increases/decreases) so opening plus net movement equals closing under the stated convention.
